title: Divide with remainders (2-digit by 1-digit)
description: Practice finding remainders in small division problems, like 24÷5.
topic_path: Get ready for multiplication and division with whole numbers and decimals

assessment_items.question_content:
**Write your answer as a whole number and remainder.**

$7 \div 3 = ~$[[☃ numeric-input 1]] $\text{R}$ [[☃ numeric-input 2]]
hints:
Let's divide $\tealD7$ circles into groups of $\goldD3$.

[[☃ image 1]]
We get $2$ groups of $\goldD3$ with $1$ circle leftover.
$7 \div 3 = 2 \text{ R }  1$

assessment_items.question_content:
**Write your answer as a whole number and remainder.**

$44\div 8 = ~$[[☃ numeric-input 1]] $\text{R}$ [[☃ numeric-input 2]]
hints:
Let's divide $\tealD{44}$ circles into groups of $\goldD8$.

[[☃ image 1]]
We get $5$ groups of $\goldD8$ with $4$ circles leftover.

We can also use long division to solve $\tealD{44}\div \goldD8$.

$\begin{align}
&~~ \,05\\
\goldD8&\overline{\Big)\tealD{44}} \phantom{0000000}& \\
&\underline{\, \mbox{-}0\!\downarrow}&\\
&\phantom{0}{44}& \\
&\underline{\, \mbox{-}40}&\\
&\phantom{00}4& \\
\end{align}$
$44 \div 8 = 5 \text{ R }  4$

assessment_items.question_content:
**Write your answer as a whole number and remainder.**

$73\div 8 = ~$[[☃ numeric-input 1]] $\text{R}$ [[☃ numeric-input 2]]
hints:
Let's divide $\tealD{73}$ circles into groups of $\goldD8$.

[[☃ image 1]]
We get $9$ groups of $\goldD8$ with $1$ circle leftover.

We can also use long division to solve $\tealD{73}\div \goldD8$.

$\begin{align}
&~~ \,09\\
\goldD8&\overline{\Big)\tealD{73}} \phantom{0000000}& \\
&\underline{\, \mbox{-}0\!\downarrow}&\\
&\phantom{0}{73}& \\
&\underline{\, \mbox{-}72}&\\
&\phantom{00}1& \\
\end{align}$
$73 \div 8 = 9 \text{ R }  1$
